title: US Treasuries Are Uninvestible
gist: Charles Gave of Gavekal argues that US Treasuries have been uninvestible for years and announces a reassessment of that long-held view. The analysis extends to UK gilts and French bonds as broadly comparable cases. He frames the current environment against the 1970s, a prior decade-long period when US bonds were also uninvestible.

insights.claim: US Treasuries, along with UK gilts and French government bonds, have been uninvestible for recent years — a view Gavekal's Charles Gave is now formally reassessing.
insights.speaker: Charles Gave
insights.so_what: A formal reassessment by a prominent macro strategist that long-duration government bonds were uninvestible signals a potential regime change in fixed-income positioning — investors should watch whether Gave concludes the trade has turned.
insights.quote: In recent years, I have maintained that the government bond markets of the United States, France and the United Kingdom have been uninvestible... The time has come to reassess that view.

insights.claim: Gavekal's thesis on uninvestible government bonds spans multiple prior publications, referencing 'Stable Financial Systems Versus Unstable Financial Systems' and 'The Euthanasia Of The Rentier' as foundational pieces.
insights.speaker: Charles Gave
insights.so_what: The view is part of a sustained, documented intellectual framework — not a reactive call — lending it structural credibility and suggesting the reassessment carries meaningful weight.

insights.claim: The reassessment will focus on the US bond market, with conclusions described as broadly similar for the UK and France.
insights.speaker: Charles Gave
insights.so_what: The parallel treatment of the UK and France suggests the uninvestibility argument is rooted in shared macro dynamics (likely debt trajectories and real yield suppression) rather than country-specific factors, widening the investment implication across DM sovereign bond markets.
insights.quote: While the discussion in this paper will focus on the US bond market, the conclusions are broadly similar for the UK and France.

insights.claim: This is not the first time in half a century that US Treasuries were uninvestible for more than a decade — the 1970s featured a decade of serial economic catastrophes that rendered them similarly uninvestible.
insights.speaker: Charles Gave
insights.so_what: Historical framing against the 1970s implies that the current episode is part of a recognizable cycle, not unprecedented — relevant for scenario analysis and duration risk assessment over multi-year horizons.
insights.quote: A look back to the 1970s reveals a decade marked by a seemingly never-ending succession of economic catastrophes in the US and beyond.
